parameterdescription.para: Angle in Q16 radians. Must not be near ±π/2.

briefdescription.para: Core atan approximation for x in [0, 1] (Q16.16).

detaileddescription.para: Uses a 7th-order minimax polynomial: atan(x) ≈ c1*x + c3*x³ + c5*x⁵ + c7*x⁷
Max error < 0.0003 rad over [0, 1]. High precision for Q16.16.

parameterdescription.para: Input value in Q16.16 (must be in [0, Q16_ONE]).
Input in Q16, must be in [−1.0, 1.0].
Input in Q16, must be in [−1.0, 1.0].

briefdescription.para: Compute floor of 32-bit unsigned integer square root: ⌊√n⌋.
Compute floor of 64-bit unsigned integer square root: ⌊√n⌋.
Fixed-point exponential e^x (range reduction + minimax polynomial).

simplesect.para: e^x in Q16. Returns INT32_MAX on overflow.

briefdescription.para: Fixed-point natural logarithm ln(x) (CLZ + minimax polynomial).
Fixed-point round: nearest integer (half rounded up).
Evaluate polynomial P(x) = c0 + c1*x + c2*x^2 + ... + cn*x^n via Horner's method.

parameterdescription.para: Array of polynomial coefficients [c0, c1, ..., cn].

memberdef.argsstring: (q16_t val, char *buf, size_t buf_len, uint8_t decimals)
memberdef.name: q16_to_str

briefdescription.para: Format a Q16 value as a decimal string (e.g. "-12.345").

detaileddescription.para: No heap allocation. The caller provides the output buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Size of output buffer in bytes.

simplesect.para: Number of characters written (excluding NUL), or 0 on error.

briefdescription.para: Parse a decimal string (e.g. "-3.1415") into Q16.

detaileddescription.para: Handles optional sign, integer part, optional decimal point and fractional digits. Stops at first non-numeric character.

simplesect.para: Number of characters consumed, or 0 on parse error.

briefdescription.para: Q16.16 fixed-point transcendental functions and string I/O.

detaileddescription.para: All functions use 64-bit intermediates for multiply chains. No floating point, no heap, no division by zero. Optimized for 32-bit ARM Cortex-M with hardware multiply; functional on 8-bit AVR.
